location.office_time_required: regular onsites and in-person meetings; most teams meet once or twice per quarter for 2-4 consecutive days at a time

secondary_archetype: Technical PM with engineering velocity and ML platform focus

level_signals: 8+ years of technical program management, product management or related experience
Comp range $187,900–$260,000 base, consistent with Principal IC at a growth-stage tech company
Expected to influence senior and executive stakeholders on ambiguous, high-impact initiatives
Expected to shape how the organization executes at scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BA/BS in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience
8+ years of technical program management, product management or related experience in a software engineering environment
Experience leading large-scale, cross-functional programs spanning multiple engineering teams and systems
Experience leading complex technical initiatives from concept through launch, including defining scope, success metrics, and execution strategy
Experience working on distributed systems, service-oriented architectures, and system design at scale

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong judgment and prioritization skills; able to navigate trade-offs with confidence and clarity across both technical and business domains.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to explain complex technical topics to diverse audiences
Track record of driving adoption of new processes, tools, or engineering standards across entire organizations
Proven ability to influence senior and executive stakeholders on ambiguous, high-impact initiatives
Experience increasing engineering velocity by driving improvements in tooling, automation, or AI-based solutions

scope.surface: Company-wide critical engineering initiatives spanning platformization, operational excellence, and ML/data evaluation productization
scope.business_model: AI lending marketplace, B2B2C (partners with banks and credit unions)
primary_metrics_inferred: Measurable business value from program delivery
core_responsibilities: Own system-level outcomes, ensuring initiatives deliver measurable business value instead of just ticking off feature completion checkboxes
Bring clarity to ambiguity by aligning goals, defining milestones, translating high-level designs into actionable implementation details, and ensuring cross-team dependencies are clearly understood
Proactively identify and mitigate critical risks early to de-risk delivery before failure happens
Drive critical decisions by surfacing tradeoffs, clarifying priorities, and aligning stakeholders on a path forward
Create and maintain a single, trusted view of program health to provide stakeholders at all levels with insights into progress, risks, and changes
Partner with engineering and product leaders to balance feature delivery with long-term system scalability, reliability, and maintainability

compensation.equity_form: annual equity grants that vest quarterly; Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) with discounted stock purchase options

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($187,900–$260,000 base) with bonus and equity mentioned
Fully remote-eligible across US and most of Canada
Clear focus areas for the TPM team (platformization, operational excellence, ML/data productization)
Principal-level ownership and influence scope clearly articulated
Generous benefits package including 401(k) match up to $15,000 annually
red_flags: Several preferred qualifications (communication, judgment, executive influence) read like must-haves in practice but are listed as preferred — ambiguity in classification
Remote role still requires regular in-person travel (quarterly onsites 2-4 consecutive days); travel burden not fully quantified
Wide compensation band ($72,100 spread) reflects significant geographic pay differential — actual offer could be significantly lower than the top of the range for non-Bay Area candidates
